macro_segments.title: Introduction to Mishna 11: Overview of Kemitzah and Structural Parallels to Zvachim

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks on Mishna 11 and parallels to Zvachim
Physical description of kemitzah (palm-down vs. sideways) and disputes over levonah amount

macro_segments.title: Pigul Thought: Wrong Place (Chutz Limkomo) versus Wrong Time (Chutz Lizmano)

micro_segments.title: Introducing pigul and the distinction between wrong place (chutz limkomo) and wrong time (chutz lizmano) thoughts
Cases of eating or burning remnants outside the Beit HaMikdash (wrong place): pasul but no kareit
Cases of eating or burning at the wrong time: pigul with kareit obligation
Definition of pigul and the four avodos from kemitzah to haktarah

macro_segments.title: Requirement of Proper Completion (Hamatir K'mitzvato) and Compound Invalidating Thoughts

micro_segments.title: The requirement that the permitting act (haktarah) be done correctly for pigul to apply
Examples where pigul succeeds: one or consistent wrong-time thoughts throughout all avodos
Examples where pigul fails: presence of non-pigul invalidating thought (chutz limkomo or shelo lishma)
Compound thought: planning to eat one k'zayis outside and one k'zayis tomorrow, or reverse order
Half-portions (kechatzayis): planning half outside and half tomorrow—insufficient for complete pigul

macro_segments.title: Requirement of Proper Completion (Hamatir K'mitzvato) and Compound Invalidating Thoughts (continued)

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yehuda's principle: whether wrong-time or wrong-place thought came first determines pigul status
Sages' position: both wrong-time and wrong-place thoughts together render it pasul without pigul

macro_segments.title: The Question of Lost Shirayim and Pigul Status

micro_segments.title: Setting up the question: can pigul apply when some shirayim are lost between kemitzah and haktarah
Rav Huna's position: if shirayim are intrinsically deficient, haktarah does not establish pigul
Rava's position: partial loss (lacking) is an intrinsic defect worse than yotzei, so pigul does not apply

macro_segments.title: Analysis of Rabbi Chiya's Baraita: Why 'Or K'zayis' Is Omitted

micro_segments.title: Rava's reading: Rabbi Chiya omits 'or k'zayis' to avoid suggesting all shirayim are lost
Abaye's reading: Rabbi Chiya follows Rabbi Eliezer, requiring full amount for haktarah to be an act
Implications of Rabbi Eliezer's position: k'zayis insufficient for act of haktarah; full amount required
Why k'zayis cannot be mentioned by eating: consistency with haktarah requirement; definition of eating act

macro_segments.title: Rava's Retraction: Chaser as Worse Than Yotzei

micro_segments.title: Rava retracts his position using Baraita on Lechem HaPanim: if one loaf breaks, all are pasul
Rava's inference: chaser is worse than yotzei because chaser invalidates all, yotzei does not
Abaye's response: Baraita may reflect Rabbi Eliezer's position; yotzei might also be problematic
Conclusion: chaser and yotzei status depends on Rabbi Akiva vs. Rabbi Eliezer debate
